urgency.message: 75 critical CVE(s) and 6 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Russian APT Deploys ‘StockStay’ Backdoor Against Ukrainian Targets
items.summary:
Turla has been using the backdoor against government and military organizations in Ukraine for espionage.
The post Russian APT Deploys ‘StockStay’ Backdoor Against Ukrainian Targets appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Russia Used Cellebrite on Jailed Activist's iPhone Months After Sales Cutoff
items.summary:
Russian authorities used Cellebrite's UFED forensic tools to break into the iPhone of detained opposition activist Andrey Pivovarov in June 2021, three months after Cellebrite said it would stop selling its tools and services to Russia and Belarus.

The finding, published June 25 by the Citizen

items.title: First-Ever Exploitation of PTC Windchill Vulnerability Discovered in the Wild
items.summary:
CISA has added the remote code execution flaw CVE-2026-12569 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
The post First-Ever Exploitation of PTC Windchill Vulnerability Discovered in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Enterprise-Ready MCP Specification Brings New Security Challenges
items.summary:
A major overhaul of the Model Context Protocol shifts critical security responsibilities from the protocol itself to developers and platform operators.
The post New Enterprise-Ready MCP Specification Brings New Security Challenges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CMC Releases Analysis and Guidance for Education Sector After Canvas Data Breach
items.summary: The UK Cyber Monitoring Centre reviews the Canvas breach affecting 160 UK universities, highlighting data theft risks and financial impacts of cyber incidents

items.title: Google Details Turla's New STOCKSTAY Backdoor Used in Ukraine Espionage Attacks
items.summary:
The Russian state-sponsored threat actor known as Turla has been attributed to a previously undocumented .NET backdoor called STOCKSTAY that has been deployed against government and military organizations in Ukraine, and entities that have an interest in Italian foreign policy.

Describing the Windo

items.title: Philip Martin Joins Uber as Chief Information Security Officer
items.summary:
Martin brings experience from Coinbase, Palantir, Amazon, and the U.S. Army to lead Uber's cybersecurity and enterprise security organization.
The post Philip Martin Joins Uber as Chief Information Security Officer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic is testing desktop-like Claude Cowork for mobile
items.summary: Anthropic appears to be testing Claude Cowork support on mobile, allowing you to manage long-running Claude tasks from your phone. [...]

items.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
items.summary: Authorities in Poland have arrested four members of an organized cybercrime group accused of breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts to carry out SIM-swapping attacks. [...]

items.title: CL-STA-1062 Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Critical Infrastructure
items.summary:
Government entities and critical infrastructure were targeted for espionage in SE Asia by attackers using a hybrid toolkit, including custom TinyRCT backdoor. 
The post CL-STA-1062 Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Critical Infrastructure appeared first on Unit 42.

items.title: In Less Than 24 Hours, Attackers Weaponize Cisco CUCM Flaw
items.summary: The flaw enables server-side request forgery (SSRF) and escalates privileges to root, impacting Cisco Unified CM and Unified CM SME deployments.

items.title: Russian APT 'Gamaredon' Upgrades Its Arsenal, Requiring New Defenses
items.summary: The FSB state-sponsored operation has gotten a lot better at loading its malware and hiding its servers.

items.title: EdTech Attackers Shift From Schools to Their Software Suppliers
items.summary: Educational institutions, the edtech companies they rely on, and, more concerningly, the challenges they pose for schools are the focus of the latest Reporters' Notebook video series.

items.title: Order-tracking app Shop abused to push callback phishing attacks
items.summary: Threat actors are increasingly abusing Shop, the order-tracking app from Shopify, by adding fake purchase receipts in users' order histories to trick them into providing sensitive data or installing remote access software. [...]

items.title: Local Police Collusion Hampers Crackdown on Asian Scam Centers
items.summary: With tens of billions of dollars flowing into regional economies from cybercrime, scam centers continue to flourish, despite international and law-enforcement efforts.

items.title: DHS chief says president has met with potential CISA nominee; agency plans to hire 600
items.summary: Once a new CISA director is in place, the agency will ramp up hiring efforts, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin told lawmakers. The White House has not yet announced a nominee.

items.title: Microsoft quietly extends free Windows 10 ESU support to October 2027
items.summary: Microsoft has quietly extended its free Windows 10 Extended Security Updates (ESU) program for consumers by an additional year, allowing enrolled devices to continue receiving security updates until October 12, 2027. [...]

items.title: Beyond IOCs: AI-enabled threat intelligence
items.summary: In this week’s newsletter, Martin considers how AI will help threat intelligence by creating an easily queryable data source of intelligence reports.

items.title: New macOS malware embeds fake errors to confuse AI analysis tools
items.summary: A newly discovered macOS malware dubbed "Gaslight" is designed to confuse AI-assisted malware analysis tools by hiding prompt injection strings and fake debugging data within the executable. [...]

items.title: PirloTV sports piracy network disrupted as 44 domains seized
items.summary: A major sports piracy ring linked to the illegal PirloTV streaming platform has been disrupted in an action that targeted 44 domains. [...]

items.title: Bluekit phishing kit adopts browser-in-the-middle for login theft
items.summary: The Bluekit phishing-as-a-service platform continues to evolve with nearly 70 new hostnames identified over the past week and by adding browser-in-the-middle capabilities for improved data theft. [...]

items.title: Another Russian dairy company reportedly disrupted by cyberattack
items.summary: A dairy products manufacturer in Russia's republic of Bashkortostan is the latest such company to have its operations snarled by a cyberattack.

items.title: Cisco Vulnerability Exploited Months Before Disclosure, Google Warns
items.summary: A high-severity flaw in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager disclosed in early June was exploited as early as March

items.title: Chrome Ad Blocker with 10M+ Installs Found with Dormant Script Injection Capability
items.summary:
An analysis of a popular Google Chrome ad block extension for YouTube has uncovered the ability to execute arbitrary JavaScript code.

According to Island, the extension, named Adblock for YouTube (ID: cmedhionkhpnakcndndgjdbohmhepckk), has more than 10 million installs and carries a Featured badge

items.title: The Four Elevations of Effective Fraud Prevention
items.summary: Fraudsters don't attack just one transaction. They target accounts, platforms, and entire ecosystems. IPQS explains the four elevations of fraud prevention and why broader visibility improves fraud detection. [...]

items.title: Twenty Million US IP Connections Used by Proxy Services
items.summary: Digital Citizens Alliance report claims that millions of Americans may have unwittingly had IP connections used by cybercriminals

items.title: Runlayer Raises $30 Million in Series A Funding
items.summary:
The startup’s platform functions as a secure control layer, aiming to secure AI tools across enterprises.
The post Runlayer Raises $30 Million in Series A Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Smart TV Proxyware, 24-Year curl Bug, AI Crime Forums + 13 More Stories
items.summary:
It’s dumb out there again.

items.title: Ukraine's state postal operator reports app disruption after cyberattack
items.summary: Ukraine's state-owned postal operator said it was experiencing disruptions to some of its app services due to a suspected cyberattack, but did not say who was behind it.

items.title: Russia used Cellebrite phone-hacking tool to crack down on dissident after firm cut off country
items.summary: The continued use of the powerful data extraction product soon after the company in March 2021 said it would stop working with Russia suggests the firm has been unable to pull back its technology from authoritarian government customers, researchers say.

items.title: Cal Water Says No OT Systems Breached in Iranian Handala Cyberattack
items.summary:
Mandiant has helped the California water utility investigate the cyberattack launched by Iranian hacker group Handala.
The post Cal Water Says No OT Systems Breached in Iranian Handala Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Trust in Automated AI Vulnerability Scanning Collapses to 9%, New Study Finds
items.summary: Cobalt study finds 20-percentage-point drop in number of organizations relying solely on AI automation for testing

items.title: New CISA Guide Helps Agencies Adopt SASE For Zero Trust
items.summary: New CISA guidance shows federal agencies how to use SASE to move from legacy TIC 2.0 to zero trust

items.title: Lantronix Serial-to-IP Converter Flaw Exploited in Attacks After OT Threat Warning
items.summary:
The exploited flaw, CVE-2025-67038, is one of the vulnerabilities disclosed in April as part of the BRIDGE:BREAK research project.
The post Lantronix Serial-to-IP Converter Flaw Exploited in Attacks After OT Threat Warning appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.summary:
Despite the abundance of telemetry at analysts’ disposal, many security operations teams struggle to answer a few basic questions during incident investigation: What happened? What evidence do we have? How do we know we’re seeing it all, in context?

items.title: GitLab Patches Code Execution, Information Disclosure Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
The latest GitLab CE/EE updates address 13 vulnerabilities, including three high-severity defects.
The post GitLab Patches Code Execution, Information Disclosure Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Major Increase in Ransomware Attacks Targeting Europe, Warns New Report
items.summary: Analysis of ransomware incidents by researchers at Black Kite found that attacks have risen by over 50% in the last year, with supply chain attacks increasing

items.summary: [This is a Guest Diary by Nicole Phillips, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu BACS program]

items.title: Introduction to COM usage by Windows threats
items.summary: Component Object Model (COM) is a fundamental Windows technology used by legitimate applications for object activation, inter-process communication, automation and language-independent component reuse. Those same qualities make it useful to threat actors.

items.title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
items.summary: After a global lull, ransomware gangs are setting sights on a rich new arena: attacking EU organizations and their suppliers.

items.title: British Police Built a Sprawling Crime-Prediction Machine. Some Results Couldn’t Be Trusted
items.summary: As UK police embrace the AI revolution, a WIRED investigation reveals the messy inside story of one region’s experiment with predictive analytics.
